key_insight: CertMix aligns exemplar metamaterial designs into a shared neural-implicit weight space, where property matching becomes a small affine-mixing problem with a certification step.
engineering_relevance: It enables fast, data-efficient inverse design of mechanical metamaterials with guaranteed property-error bounds, reducing reliance on expensive per-target topology optimization.
method: Represent each exemplar unit cell as a SIREN signed-distance field overfit from a shared anchor to align weight vectors.
Solve target design by affine mixing of aligned weights with a differentiable periodic homogenizer in the loop.
Use a trust region plus split-conformal calibration to keep blends valid and certify achieved-property error.
evidence_signal: With only 50 exemplars, the method reaches scaled property error of 10^-4, is far outside the exemplar range, and is reported as 57× faster than per-target topology optimization.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well elasticity remains approximately linear in the aligned weight space, so extrapolation and certification may degrade if the exemplar set is too sparse or poorly representative.
paper_title: CertMix: Certified, Data-Efficient Metamaterial Design by Affine Mixing of Aligned Neural-Implicit Weight Spaces
